Hero.eyebrow: Sole Designer at Axel (YC W19) · Bellevue, WA
Hero.bio: Designer who codes — shipping product design and production React at <axel>Axel (YC W19)</axel>, solo and fast.

SelectedWork.viewAll: View all projects on Aspen Lab →

axel.title: Axel — sole designer × bidirectional loop
axel.summary: Sole designer at a YC W19 travel-money startup, reporting directly to the CEO. Built a symmetric Design ⇌ FE delivery protocol with the lead engineer that turns handoff into convergence — three zones, four review tiers, a weekly cadence. Every pixel in Axel goes through one person.

nutra.title: Nutra — fintech for everyone
nutra.summary: A budgeting app for the rest of us. Gamified, friendly, built for people who want to save but can't sustain it. 85% of testers reported higher motivation; 90% loved the play of it.

tiktok.title: TikTok Pay KYC, scaled across markets
tiktok.summary: KYC across Vietnam, Malaysia, Indonesia, and the US for TikTok Pay, Shop, and Live. Vietnam new-user completion 70% → 90%. Vendor docking SOP cut integration cycles by ~40%.

hyundai.title: Hyundai IONIQ 6 — designing for trust at L2+
hyundai.summary: How might we improve driver confidence in semi-autonomous scenarios, where control transitions between human and machine? A study across 9 drivers, biometrics, and three UI directions — Green UI lifted confidence by +10% over the baseline.

cone.title: Field of Vision — wearable for visually impaired pedestrians
cone.summary: A wearable + interface system designed for visually impaired pedestrians. Winner of both iF Design Award and Red Dot Design Award in 2025.

TechStack.intro: Designer who codes. Most of my side projects ship to production — mostly written out of <ink>Cursor</ink> and <ink>Claude Code</ink>, with a chain of MCPs gluing Figma, Unity, and Customer.io into the same loop.
TechStack.spectrumNote: All five lit. The width is the point — the moat is the AI loop that fuses design, frontend, email, backend, and game dev into a single delivery chain.

SideProjects.intro: Things I build between design jobs — usually to scratch a personal itch, sometimes to test a tool I'm thinking about adopting at work.

calendar.activeDays: {count} active days · peak {peak} in a day

Moat.intro: I'm not selling a skill — I'm selling a shape. Six credentials — proven, not asserted.

About.bio1: I'm a dual-degree student at <ink>Georgia Tech</ink> — BS Industrial Design (2021–2024) and BS Psychology (2023–2026). Design and psychology stuck together because the questions overlap: small frictions, hidden expectations, the gap between what someone says and what they do.
About.bio2: I've designed multi-country KYC for <ink>TikTok Pay</ink>, semi-autonomous HMI for <ink>Hyundai</ink>, a packaging system for the <ink>CDC</ink>, and co-founded <ink>XING Art</ink> with a $300K pre-seed from MiraclePlus and 1K+ real users. I'm now a Design Engineer at <ink>Axel</ink> (Gordian Software, YC W19) — shipping production React on helloaxel.com, building the transactional email system on Customer.io, and reporting directly to the CEO.
About.bio3: Off the clock — film washing by hand, B&W sketching, cooking for me and my partner, building wireless illuminated dice with roommates. I move fast, hold strong opinions on type and motion, and like the part of design where research, growth, and craft intersect.
About.closingQuote: I'm waiting for you to find my potential.

Contact.intro: Pick the channel that suits you — direct line, scheduled chat, or the quick form below. {reply}.

Footer.nowText: Design Engineer at <ink>Axel</ink> (Gordian, YC W19) in Bellevue.

NotFound.body: Maybe it never shipped, maybe I moved it. Either way — the way back is one click.
